title: Annealing method for iron core
abstract:
PURPOSE: To attain a uniform temp. distribution over the entire part of iron cores in annealing by disposing heat treatment compensation bodies to both end faces of the laminated iron cores in an induction heating device in such a manner that the directing directions of plate materials of said bodies face the same direction as the lamination direction of the iron cores, then subjecting the iron cores to induction heating.
CONSTITUTION: The heat treatment compensation body 15 is formed by winding plural times a long-sized plate material 15a made of a magnetic material (ferritic stainless steel). The compensation bodies 15, 15 are disposed to both the top and bottom ends of the iron cores 14 in the induction heating device 11 in such a manner that the directing directions of plate materials 15a, 15a face the same direction as the lamination direction D of the iron cores 14. The induction heating device 11 is then operated to apply high frequencies to the iron cores 14, by which the induction heating is executed. The radiation of heat from both the top and bottom ends of the iron cores is prevented and the magnetic flux passing the iron cores pass the respective bodies 15; therefore, the disturbance of the magnetic flux is prevented. The entire part of the laminated iron cores is annealed at the uniform temp. and the generation of the defective product is obviated by the above-mentioned method.

claims:
[Claim 1]
an induction heating device when annealing the laminated iron core, and the core at both ends of the induction heating device, comprising a body made of magnetic material and the plate in a predetermined direction and directing heat compensation, and in the same direction as the orientation direction of the laminated iron plates so arranged state, and to perform the induction heating and annealing of the core.
